title: Calling `hashlib.shake_128()._keccak_init` with invalid object aborts
author: devdanzin
body:
It's possible to abort PyPy with the message `free(): invalid next size (fast)` by calling `hashlib.shake_128()._keccak_init(())`:

```python
import hashlib

hashlib.shake_128()._keccak_init(())
```

`pypy -VV`:
```
Python 3.10.14 (5cccf8d934d7, Nov 17 2024, 04:47:11)
[PyPy 7.3.18-alpha0 with GCC 10.2.1 20210130 (Red Hat 10.2.1-11)]
```

Found using fusil by @vstinner.
